name: python3-priority
title: python3-priority
summary: pure-Python implementation of the HTTP/2 priority tree (Python 3)
description:
pure-Python implementation of the HTTP/2 priority tree (Python 3)
Priority is a pure-Python implementation of the priority logic for HTTP/2, set
out in RFC 7540 Section 5.3 (Stream Priority). This logic allows for clients
to express a preference for how the server allocates its (limited) resources to
the many outstanding HTTP requests that may be running over a single HTTP/2
connection.
.
Specifically, this Python implementation uses a variant of the implementation
used in the H2O project. This original implementation is also the
inspiration for nghttp2's priority implementation, and generally produces a
very clean and even priority stream. The only notable changes from H2O's
implementation are small modifications to allow the priority implementation to
work cleanly as a separate implementation, rather than being embedded in a
HTTP/2 stack directly.
.
While priority information in HTTP/2 is only a suggestion, rather than an
enforceable constraint, where possible servers should respect the priority
requests of their clients.
.
This package is for Python 3.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use python3-priority because pure-Python implementation of the HTTP/2 priority tree (Python 3).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use python3-priority as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair python3-priority with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `python3-priority --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
